title: The Ghost-Path Divergence

features.name: divergent posts
features.desc-on-touch: two fence posts mark where the paths split — one goes left toward the valley, one right toward the ridge — but you can see footprints in both directions, seasons old, and you're standing at the place where you chose

features.name: the untaken station
features.desc-on-touch: a marker for the seven-station pilgrimage walk: you know this was the second station, where the walker rested and thought about refusal — your footprints never reached it, but the earth has kept a record of the moment you nearly turned left

features.name: grid that measures both
features.desc-on-touch: a coordinate system painted on stone: the path you took runs true through the grid, but so does the path you didn't — both are equally marked, equally real to the earth's accounting

features.name: doubt
features.desc-on-touch: a small hollow in the earth shaped like a boot — but which boot? which path? the soil shows wear in two directions and keeps neither memory clean

features.name: the fork that won't choose
features.desc-on-touch: a tree at the divergence point — its growth rings show the exact year you chose — but the heartwood is split, as if both choices are being lived at once, just in different grain directions

inscription: you chose once, but the earth keeps score of twice; the grid measures both the path you take and the one that still waits in the field

file.name: Divergence_Station_Field_Record_1994.txt
file.note: a walker's account of reaching the second station of the sacred topography, where fatherhood and refusal meet — the entry dated twice, in two different handwritings, as if two people kept the same jou

reverie.title: REVERIE — the field where both paths open
narr: you reached the fork at dusk. she had already chosen,
but waited for you anyway. the left path went toward
the ridge where you could see for forty kilometres.
the right path went down, into the scrub, into the dark.
she asked: 'which way do you want to walk?' but you
both knew the earth was keeping score of both at once.

exits.label: along the ghost-path where the other footprints lead
toward the ridge where the path becomes visible from above
into the scrub where the untaken station still waits
down to where the earth stops keeping two scores
